text: Charles Coverdale. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Coverdale C (2026). fred: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/charlescoverdale/fred.
code: @Manual{,   title = {fred: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data},   author = {Charles Coverdale},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/charlescoverdale/fred}, }

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: fred provides clean, tidy access economic data Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) API directly R.

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) database maintained Federal Reserve Bank St. Louis - one twelve regional Reserve Banks US Federal Reserve System. Fed’s Board Governors Washington sets monetary policy, regional banks developed research specialisms. St. Louis carved reputation applied economic research data dissemination, FRED launched 1991 dial-bulletin board moving web mid-1990s. since grown arguably single important free data platform macroeconomic research, now hosting 800,000 time series covering GDP, employment, inflation, interest rates, trade, financial markets, . makes FRED unusual role aggregator. database doesn’t just publish St. Louis Fed’s data - pulls series 118 different sources, including Bureau Labor Statistics, Bureau Economic Analysis, Census Bureau, Treasury Department, World Bank, dozens US international agencies. Rather visiting agency’s website individually navigating different data formats, FRED gives single, consistent interface . ’s closest thing economics universal data catalogue.

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: FRED provides free REST API gives programmatic access full database. API supports searching series, fetching observations, browsing category tree, tracking data releases revisions, applying server-side transformations like percent change frequency aggregation - without needing visit website. Anyone can register free API key. package wraps FRED API can pull data directly R single function call. Rather constructing API URLs hand, fred_series("GDP") fetches data, parses response, returns tidy data frame. can fetch multiple series , apply transformations, search keyword, browse categories, track revisions - R. Results cached locally repeated calls instant.

what: How is this different from fredr?
title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: existing R package called fredr, written Sam Boysel Davis Vaughan published CRAN 2021. ’s solid package, hasn’t updated since August 2021. ’s better users: Multiple series one call - fredr can fetch one series time, pulling GDP, unemployment, CPI means three separate requests manually binding results. fred, fred_series(c("GDP", "UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL")) returns everything one tidy data frame. Local caching - fredr downloads data fresh every time run script. ’re knitting Quarto report tweaking chart title, re-downloads everything scratch every render. fred caches results locally first download, subsequent calls instant don’t touch API. Automatic pagination - FRED’s API returns results pages 1,000. category 2,500 series, fredr gives first 1,000 stops - users won’t realise ’re getting incomplete data. fred automatically fetches pages stitches together. ’s better hood: Modern HTTP stack - fredr depends httr, superseded httr2. fred uses httr2 built-rate-limit retries graceful error handling API unreachable. Fewer dependencies - fred depends 3 packages (httr2, cli, tools). fredr pulls httr, jsonlite, rlang, tibble, purrr. Actively maintained - fredr updates since August 2021.

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
code: install.packages("fred")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/fred")

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: need free API key use package. FRED API requires authentication St. Louis Fed can manage server load - key completely free takes two minutes set . need .

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: Go https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/login/secure/ Click Create New Account Fill name, email, password, click Create Account Check email click verification link

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: logged , go https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys Click Request API Key Enter short description (e.g. “R data analysis”) agree terms API key appear screen - ’s string letters numbers like abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890 Copy

what: Step 3: Save the key so R can find it
title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: best approach store key file called .Renviron, R reads automatically every time starts. means set never think . Option : RStudio Open RStudio console, type file.edit("~/.Renviron") press Enter - opens (creates) file Add line, replacing placeholder actual key: Save file close Restart R (Session → Restart R, press Ctrl+Shift+F10 / Cmd+Shift+F10) Option B: terminal Open Terminal (Mac/Linux) Command Prompt (Windows) Run command, replacing placeholder actual key: Restart R Option C: Set current session just want try things without editing files, can set key temporarily: lasts close R - ’ll need run next session.
code: FRED_API_KEY=abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890 echo 'FRED_API_KEY=abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890' >> ~/.Renviron library(fred) fred_set_key("abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890")

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: restarting R, check key picked : prints key, ’re good go.
code: library(fred) fred_get_key() #> [1] "abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890"

what: How do I find the series I need?
title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: Every dataset FRED short series ID. example, GDP US quarterly GDP, UNRATE US unemployment rate, CPIAUCSL Consumer Price Index. need know series ID pull data. two ways find : 1. Search R using fred_search(): 2. Browse FRED website fred.stlouisfed.org. Search browse category, series ID shown top every data page. often easiest way explore ’s available ’re sure ’re looking . series ID, pass fred_series().
code: fred_search("consumer price index") #>            id                                          title frequency #>      CPIAUCSL   Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers   Monthly #>    CPILFESL     CPI Less Food and Energy (Core CPI)          Monthly #>    CPIENGSL     CPI: Energy                                  Monthly #>    ...

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
code: library(fred)  # US quarterly GDP gdp <- fred_series("GDP") tail(gdp) #>          date series_id    value #>   2023-04-01       GDP 27063.01 #>   2023-07-01       GDP 27610.55 #>   ...

what: Fetch multiple series in one call
title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
code: # GDP, unemployment, and CPI together macro <- fred_series(c("GDP", "UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL"))  # Returns a single data frame with a series_id column table(macro$series_id) #>  CPIAUCSL       GDP    UNRATE #>       977       310       953

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: FRED can compute transformations server-side, don’t calculate .
code: # Year-on-year percent change in GDP gdp_growth <- fred_series("GDP", units = "pc1") tail(gdp_growth, 4) #>          date series_id value #>   2023-01-01       GDP  3.57 #>   2023-04-01       GDP  4.49 #>   2023-07-01       GDP  5.36 #>   2023-10-01       GDP  5.78  # Other options: "pch" (period-on-period %), "chg" (level change), # "log" (natural log), "pca" (annualised rate)

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
code: # CPI since 2020 only cpi <- fred_series("CPIAUCSL", from = "2020-01-01") head(cpi, 4) #>          date series_id   value #>   2020-01-01  CPIAUCSL  257.971 #>   2020-02-01  CPIAUCSL  258.678 #>   2020-03-01  CPIAUCSL  258.115 #>   2020-04-01  CPIAUCSL  256.389

what: Aggregate daily data to a lower frequency
title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
code: # The 10-year Treasury yield is published daily. # Aggregate to monthly averages: rates <- fred_series("DGS10", frequency = "m") tail(rates, 4) #>          date series_id value #>   2024-09-01     DGS10  3.73 #>   2024-10-01     DGS10  4.10 #>   2024-11-01     DGS10  4.34 #>   2024-12-01     DGS10  4.39  # Or get end-of-period values instead of averages: rates_eop <- fred_series("DGS10", frequency = "m", aggregation = "eop")

what: Look up what a series actually measures
title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
code: fred_info("UNRATE") #>       id                                title frequency units  seasonal_adjustment #>   UNRATE   Unemployment Rate                   Monthly     %   Seasonally Adjusted

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: multi-series analysis ’s often easier one column per series. Pass format = "wide": header top one-line summary query (series count, observations, transform, frequency, vintage). Downstream code can treat result normal data frame.
code: macro <- fred_series(c("GDP", "UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL"),                      from = "2020-01-01", format = "wide") head(macro) #> # FRED: 3 series · 60 obs · format=wide #>         date       GDP UNRATE CPIAUCSL #>   2020-01-01  21561.14    3.5  259.037 #>   2020-02-01        NA    3.5  259.250 #>   2020-03-01  21289.27    4.4  258.115 #>   ...

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: raw FRED units codes (pch, pc1, cca, etc.) work fine, ’re always memorable. can pass transform = instead: Available aliases: level, raw, diff, change, yoy_diff, qoq_pct, mom_pct, pop_pct, yoy_pct, annualised, qoq_annualised, log, log_diff, log_diff_annualised. units transform arguments mutually exclusive.
code: # Year-on-year percent change in CPI fred_series("CPIAUCSL", transform = "yoy_pct")  # Continuously compounded annual rate fred_series("GDP", transform = "log_diff_annualised")  # Quarter-on-quarter level change fred_series("GDP", transform = "diff")

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: FRED keeps full revision history series via sister database, ALFRED. fred exposes four helpers, add realtime_start realtime_end columns returned data frame. building blocks pseudo-real-time forecasting backtests revision studies. cache separately default (latest-vintage) cache, calling fred_series("GDP") fred_as_of("GDP", "2020-03-01") keep distinct cache entries.
code: # 1. The series as it appeared on a specific date gdp_then <- fred_as_of("GDP", date = "2020-03-01")  # 2. Only the first release of each observation, never revised gdp_first <- fred_first_release("GDP", from = "2010-01-01")  # 3. Every vintage in the revision history #    (potentially large - narrow with from/to for long series) gdp_all <- fred_all_vintages("GDP", from = "2020-01-01")  # 4. A panel of selected vintage snapshots gdp_panel <- fred_real_time_panel(   "GDP",   vintages = c("2020-04-30", "2020-07-31", "2020-10-31", "2021-01-31") )

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: clear_cache() wipes everything; fred_cache_info() lets check ’s .
code: fred_cache_info() #> $dir #> [1] "/Users/.../R/fred/cache" #> #> $n_files #> [1] 27 #> #> $size_human #> [1] "412.3 KB" #> #> $files #>                                  name size_bytes            modified #>   obs_GDP_lin_avg.rds                    18234   2026-04-09 11:02:13 #>   obs_UNRATE_lin_avg.rds                 16711   2026-04-09 11:02:14 #>   ...

title: Access Federal Reserve Economic Data
text: package part family R packages economic financial data. share consistent interface - named functions, tidy data frames, local caching - designed work together.

title: Clear the fred cache — clear_cache
text: Deletes locally cached FRED data files. next call data function re-download FRED API.

title: Clear the fred cache — clear_cache
code: clear_cache()

title: Clear the fred cache — clear_cache
text: Invisible NULL.

title: Clear the fred cache — clear_cache
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) clear_cache() #> Cache cleared. options(op) # }

what: fred: Access 'Federal Reserve Economic Data' — fred-package
title: fred: Access 'Federal Reserve Economic Data' — fred-package
text: Provides clean, tidy access economic data 'Federal Reserve Economic Data' ('FRED') API https://fred.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/fred/. 'FRED' maintained 'Federal Reserve Bank St. Louis' contains 800,000 time series 118 sources covering GDP, employment, inflation, interest rates, trade, . Dedicated functions fetch series observations, search series, browse categories, releases, tags, retrieve series metadata. Multiple series can fetched single call, long wide format. Server-side unit transformations (percent change, log, etc.) frequency aggregation supported, readable transform aliases 'yoy_pct' 'log_diff'. Real-time vintage helpers (built 'ALFRED') return series appeared given date, first-release version, every revision, panel selected vintages. Data cached locally subsequent calls. product uses 'FRED' API endorsed certified 'Federal Reserve Bank St. Louis'.

title: fred: Access 'Federal Reserve Economic Data' — fred-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

what: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
title: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
text: Returns full revision history: one row per (observation date, realtime range) combination. FRED API's output_type = 2 mode. result can reshaped vintage matrix used compute revision statistics.

title: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
code: fred_all_vintages(   series_id,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   units = "lin",   frequency = NULL,   aggregation = "avg",   cache = TRUE )

title: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
text: series_id Character. One FRED series IDs. , Optional observation date range. units Character. Raw FRED units code. Default "lin". frequency, aggregation Optional frequency aggregation arguments (see fred_series()). cache Logical. Cache results locally. Default TRUE.

title: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
text: fred_tbl columns date, series_id, value, realtime_start, realtime_end. realtime_start realtime_end columns identify vintage window row.

title: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
text: aware series hundreds thousands vintage rows, consider narrowing date range /long-running indicators like GDP.

title: Fetch every vintage of a series — fred_all_vintages
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) # All vintages of recent GDP releases gdp_vint <- fred_all_vintages("GDP", from = "2020-01-01") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>. options(op) # }

what: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
title: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
text: Returns values available FRED date, subsequent revisions. standard real-time data access pattern: set realtime_start = realtime_end = date. Useful backtesting forecasting models data actually available time, revised series see today.

title: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
code: fred_as_of(   series_id,   date,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   units = "lin",   frequency = NULL,   aggregation = "avg",   cache = TRUE )

title: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
text: series_id Character. One FRED series IDs. date Character Date. vintage date ("YYYY-MM-DD"). , Optional observation date range. units Character. Raw FRED units code. Default "lin". frequency, aggregation Optional frequency aggregation arguments (see fred_series()). cache Logical. Cache results locally. Default TRUE.

title: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
text: fred_tbl columns date, series_id, value, realtime_start, realtime_end.

title: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
text: Underneath, calls series/observations endpoint realtime parameters set. Results cached separately default (latest-vintage) cache, calling fred_series("GDP") fred_as_of("GDP", "2020-01-15") keep distinct cache entries.

title: Fetch a series as it appeared on a given vintage date — fred_as_of
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) # GDP as it looked on 1 March 2020 gdp_2020 <- fred_as_of("GDP", "2020-03-01") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>. options(op) # }

what: Inspect the local fred cache — fred_cache_info
title: Inspect the local fred cache — fred_cache_info
text: Returns information local cache: lives, many files contains, much disk space take. Useful debugging stale results deciding whether call clear_cache().

title: Inspect the local fred cache — fred_cache_info
code: fred_cache_info()

title: Inspect the local fred cache — fred_cache_info
text: list elements dir, n_files, size_bytes, size_human, files (data frame name, size_bytes, modified columns). Returns shape zero counts cache directory yet exist.

title: Inspect the local fred cache — fred_cache_info
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) fred_cache_info() #> $dir #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmp5ixIyW" #>  #> $n_files #> [1] 1 #>  #> $size_bytes #> [1] 4096 #>  #> $size_human #> [1] "4.0 MB" #>  #> $files #>      name size_bytes            modified #> 1 downlit       4096 2026-04-24 09:04:21 #>  options(op) # }

title: Get a FRED category — fred_category
text: Returns information single category. FRED category tree starts category 0 (root) branches 8 top-level categories: Money, Banking & Finance; Population, Employment & Labor Markets; National Accounts; Production & Business Activity; Prices; International Data; U.S. Regional Data; Academic Data.

title: Get a FRED category — fred_category
code: fred_category(category_id = 0L)

title: Get a FRED category — fred_category
text: category_id Integer. category ID. Default 0 (root).

title: Get a FRED category — fred_category
text: data frame category metadata.

title: Get a FRED category — fred_category
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) # Root category fred_category() #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # National Accounts (category 32992) fred_category(32992) #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>. options(op) # }

what: Fetch a real-time panel of a series across selected vintages — fred_real_time_panel
title: Fetch a real-time panel of a series across selected vintages — fred_real_time_panel

Default "lin". frequency, aggregation Optional frequency aggregation arguments (see fred_series()). cache Logical. Cache results locally. Default TRUE.

what: Make a raw request to the FRED API — fred_request
title: Make a raw request to the FRED API — fred_request
text: Low-level function sends request FRED API endpoint returns parsed JSON list. users use higher-level functions fred_series() fred_search().

title: Make a raw request to the FRED API — fred_request
code: fred_request(endpoint, ...)

title: Make a raw request to the FRED API — fred_request
text: endpoint Character. API endpoint path (e.g. "series/observations"). ... Named parameters passed query string arguments API.

title: Make a raw request to the FRED API — fred_request
text: list parsed JSON response.

title: Make a raw request to the FRED API — fred_request
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) fred_request("series", series_id = "GDP") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>. options(op) # }

title: Search for FRED series — fred_search
text: Searches FRED database keywords series ID substring. Returns matching series metadata, ordered relevance.

title: Search for FRED series — fred_search
code: fred_search(   query,   type = "full_text",   limit = 100L,   order_by = "search_rank",   filter_variable = NULL,   filter_value = NULL,   tag_names = NULL )

title: Search for FRED series — fred_search
text: query Character. Search terms (e.g. "GDP", "unemployment rate"). type Character. Either "full_text" (default) keyword search "series_id" series ID substring matching (supports * wildcards). limit Integer. Maximum number results return. Default 100, maximum 1000. order_by Character. order results. One "search_rank" (default), "series_id", "title", "units", "frequency", "seasonal_adjustment", "last_updated", "popularity", "group_popularity". filter_variable Character. Optional variable filter . One "frequency", "units", "seasonal_adjustment". filter_value Character. value filter (e.g. "Monthly", "Quarterly"). Required filter_variable specified. tag_names Character. Optional comma-separated tag names filter results (e.g. "gdp", "usa;quarterly").

title: Search for FRED series — fred_search
text: data frame matching series columns including id, title, frequency, units, seasonal_adjustment, last_updated, popularity, notes.

title: Search for FRED series — fred_search
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) # Keyword search fred_search("unemployment rate") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # Filter to monthly series only fred_search("consumer price index", filter_variable = "frequency",             filter_value = "Monthly") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # Search by series ID pattern fred_search("GDP*", type = "series_id") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>. options(op) # }

what: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
title: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
text: main function package. Downloads time series observations FRED returns tidy data frame. Multiple series can fetched single call, either long wide format.

title: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
code: fred_series(   series_id,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   units = "lin",   transform = NULL,   frequency = NULL,   aggregation = "avg",   format = c("long", "wide"),   cache = TRUE )

title: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
text: series_id Character. One FRED series IDs (e.g. "GDP", c("GDP", "UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL")). Optional start date. Character ("YYYY-MM-DD") Date. Optional end date. Character ("YYYY-MM-DD") Date. units Character. Raw FRED units code. Default "lin" (levels). Mutually exclusive transform. transform Character. Readable transformation name. See Details. frequency Character. Frequency aggregation. One "d" (daily), "w" (weekly), "bw" (biweekly), "m" (monthly), "q" (quarterly), "sa" (semiannual), "" (annual), NULL (native frequency, default). aggregation Character. Aggregation method frequency specified. One "avg" (default), "sum", "eop" (end period). format Character. "long" (default) returns one row per (series_id, date). "wide" returns one row per date one column per series. cache Logical. TRUE (default), results cached locally returned cache subsequent calls. Set FALSE force fresh download API.

title: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
text: fred_tbl (data.frame subclass prints one-line provenance header). long format, columns date, series_id, value. wide format, columns date plus one numeric column per series.

title: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
text: FRED supports server-side unit transformations via units argument. avoids need compute growth rates log transforms locally. readability can pass transform instead units: "level", "raw" -levels (default) "diff", "change" -change previous period "yoy_diff" -change one year ago "qoq_pct", "mom_pct", "pop_pct" -percent change previous period "yoy_pct" -percent change one year ago "annualised", "qoq_annualised" -compounded annual rate change "log" -natural log "log_diff" -continuously compounded rate change "log_diff_annualised" -continuously compounded annual rate Raw FRED units codes ("lin", "chg", "ch1", "pch", "pc1", "pca", "cch", "cca", "log") also accepted.

title: Fetch observations for one or more FRED series — fred_series
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(fred.cache_dir = tempdir()) # Single series gdp <- fred_series("GDP") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # Multiple series, long format macro <- fred_series(c("GDP", "UNRATE", "CPIAUCSL")) #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # Multiple series, wide format macro_w <- fred_series(c("GDP", "UNRATE"), format = "wide") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # Readable transformation: year-on-year percent change gdp_growth <- fred_series("GDP", transform = "yoy_pct") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>.  # Aggregate daily to monthly rates <- fred_series("DGS10", frequency = "m") #> Error in fred_get_key(): No FRED API key found. #> ℹ Set one with `fred_set_key()` or the `FRED_API_KEY` environment variable. #> ℹ Register for a free key at <https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys>. options(op) # }

text: Invisible NULL.

text: Register free API key https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fred_set_key("your_api_key_here") } # }

title: fred 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-11

title: fred 0.2.0
text: fred_series() gains format argument. Pass format = "wide" get one row per date column per series, instead default long layout. fred_series() gains transform argument readable aliases FRED units codes: "level", "diff", "yoy_diff", "qoq_pct", "yoy_pct", "annualised", "log", "log_diff", . raw units codes still work; transform units mutually exclusive. fred_as_of() returns series appeared chosen vintage date. fred_first_release() returns initial release observation, subsequent revisions. fred_all_vintages() returns full revision history. fred_real_time_panel() returns values available chosen set vintage dates. New fred_cache_info() reports cache directory, file count, total size, per-file metadata. Useful debugging stale results. observation results now returned fred_tbl, thin data.frame subclass one-line provenance header showing query (series count, observation count, units, transform, frequency, vintage). header informational; downstream code can keep treating result plain data frame.

title: fred 0.2.0
text: cache key format unchanged default arguments, caches built 0.1.x still picked upgrade. Realtime, output-type, vintage requests cached distinct keys.

title: fred 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-19 Removed non-existent pkgdown URL DESCRIPTION.

title: fred 0.1.1
text: Examples now use \donttest tempdir() cache instead \dontrun, fixing CRAN policy compliance. Cache directory now configurable via options(fred.cache_dir = ...).

title: fred 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. Core function fred_series() fetching one FRED time series. Server-side unit transformations frequency aggregation. Local caching downloaded data. Search, category, release, source, tag, vintage endpoints.
